findings.message: DeleteButton triggers an irreversible destructive action with no confirmation step. A misclick permanently removes the item with no way to recover.
findings.suggestion: Show a confirmation dialog (e.g. window.confirm or a modal) before issuing the DELETE request, or provide an undo/soft-delete mechanism.

findings.message: The button has no disabled or loading state while the DELETE request is in flight. Users can click multiple times, firing duplicate requests, and receive no visual indication that work is in progress.
findings.suggestion: Add a boolean `isPending` state, set it true before the fetch and false (or skip reset since the page reloads) after. Disable the button and show a 'Deleting…' label while pending.

findings.message: `window.location.reload()` is called unconditionally after the fetch, including when the server responds with a 4xx or 5xx status. `fetch()` only rejects on network failure, not HTTP error codes, so a failed delete silently reloads the page as if it succeeded — leaving the item still present with no error communicated to the user.
findings.suggestion: Check `response.ok` after the fetch; only reload on success and display an error message (e.g. set an error state rendered near the button) on failure.
